title: Writing - Part 1: Online Shopping
description: Your class has been discussing the impact of online shopping on society.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the impacts in your notes. You should explain which impact you think is more significant, giving reasons for your opinion.

impacts: Damage to local high street shops
opinions: Shopping online wastes packaging and increases pollution from delivery vans.
Small local shops cannot compete with online prices.
Consumers should have the freedom to shop wherever they choose.

structure.introduction: Introduce online shopping and the two impacts you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first impact (e.g., convenience for consumers) and its positive aspects.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second impact (e.g., damage to local shops) and its negative consequences.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both impacts and state which you think is more significant.

content.modelAnswer:
Online shopping has revolutionised the way people buy goods and has grown enormously over the past decade. While it brings obvious benefits, it also raises important concerns for communities and the environment. This essay will consider the convenience it offers to consumers and its damaging effect on local high street shops.

The convenience of online shopping is undeniable. Consumers can browse millions of products, compare prices and make purchases at any time of day without leaving their homes. For busy people or those with limited mobility, this flexibility is particularly valuable. Home delivery services have made the experience even more seamless and efficient.

However, the rise of online retail has had a devastating impact on traditional shops. Many small businesses and large retailers alike have been forced to close, as they struggle to compete with the lower prices and wider choice available online. This has left many town centres looking empty and has led to job losses in the retail sector.

In conclusion, while the convenience of online shopping is a genuine advantage, I believe its negative impact on local businesses is the more significant issue. Vibrant high streets are vital for communities, and once these businesses are lost, they are very difficult to replace. Consumers should be encouraged to support local shops where possible.
